what_it_does: Records a Tim-direct decision to absorb the capabilities and design principles of the WILDCARD project into the company while discarding its experimental front-end code, listing what to keep, what to discard, and why it converges with other company systems.
observations: YAML frontmatter includes id, address, type=idea, source=claude_code, state=captured, title, author_session, timestamps, and a history transition.
Body is a dated TIM-DIRECT memo (2026-06-18) flagging WILDCARD as a source to mine, not an immediate build.
Keep list: CONTEXT ENVELOPE, ANNOTATION system + ELEMENT IDs, SUPABASE backend, extended-markdown/React rendering.
Discard list: the actual front-end and the code currently running it.
Claims convergence with decision-surface mark contract, gallery review loop, and RUNG-C rendering.
Mentions DNA channel ch-ovxwz8k8 and relationships to a fork/merge.
standouts: Explicit keep/discard split between WILDCARD's reusable capabilities/design and its experimental front-end code.
Treats the annotation + element-ID + context-envelope model as a shared substrate across decision-surface, gallery review, and RUNG-C rendering.
Filed as a captured idea / source-to-mine rather than an active build task.
concerns: Convergence claims reference external systems (decision-surface, gallery, RUNG-C, DNA ch-ovxwz8k8) without evidence verifiable from this file alone.
No explicit owner, priority, or concrete next-action beyond 'not a build now'.
notes: A captured design-intent memo flagging WILDCARD capabilities to mine and its front-end to discard, with pointers to related company systems.
questions: What is the exact technical shape of the CONTEXT ENVELOPE?
Which Supabase tables or schemas are already set up?
What is the precise fork/merge status with the decision-surface mark contract?
What specifically distinguishes the experimental front-end from the reusable rendering machinery?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to record a source-to-mine design decision so it is not lost; the file does exactly that, so purpose matches actual.
apparent_themes: Design lineage and convergence across systems
Recorded intent as a future build trigger
Separation of experimental front-end from underlying substrate
intention_signals: The file is trying to preserve a high-level architectural directive: capture WILDCARD's reusable concepts and persistence patterns, avoid carrying forward its experimental UI, and align those concepts with the company's existing mark/address and rendering layers.
novelty: It frames a project not as code to merge but as a 'source to mine' for capabilities, explicitly separating design DNA from front-end implementation.
contribution: It contributes a recorded decision boundary and an inventory of WILDCARD capabilities (context envelope, annotation/element IDs, Supabase backend, extended rendering) that a fused system could consult when building or aligning mark/address and rendering layers.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard idea item recording a directive to absorb the WILDCARD project's context-envelope, annotation, element-ID, Supabase, and rendering capabilities while discarding its experimental front-end, noting convergence with decision-surface, gallery review, and RUNG-C rendering.
intention_for_embedding: To preserve a Tim-direct design decision and source-to-mine pointer for aligning WILDCARD's reusable substrate with the company's mark/address and rendering systems.
